mentions.note: Traditional enterprise software facing pressure from AI-powered alternatives; pricing power under threat as customers explore cheaper custom AI solutions.
#2 worst January, down ~25%. TurboTax/QuickBooks maker facing AI displacement fears. Enterprise software malaise; multiple compression ongoing.
Down 11%, worst performer in software cohort; no natural defenses, minimal dividends, few buybacks; vulnerability in AI-disruption narrative.
TurboTax and QuickBooks; down 45%, 19x P/E; consumer tax AI threat minimal, SMB software can't be replicated in-house by AI—baby thrown out with bathwater; buyer now.
TurboTax owner; eviscerated over 6 months on AI tax automation fears; legitimate threat but multiple compression extreme.

17% revenue growth, high-teen growth, big earnings beat, monster $2B assisted-tax segment up 45% YoY. AI-human expertise hybrid model (domain-specific compliance) differentiates vs. Claude; regulated environment requires accuracy humans alone can provide. Mid-market +40%, contracts +50% QoQ. Tax season tracking strong.

Tax/accounting software down 35% Q1; Cramer willing to stick neck out against displacement—brand/trust/expert network defensible; AI platforms lack these; stock rallied 8% since CEO Sasan Goodarzi spoke Feb 26; deserves comeback but needs time (people written it off).
Down 5% despite tax-season tailwind. Persistent selling pressure from SaaS/AI concerns overrides seasonal strength; not cheap despite valuation.
Tax/financial software caught up in broader enterprise software selloff; down 7% despite tax season, showing rotation intensity.
